en_to_linkedin: I got laid off from Google last week.
I was passed over for the promotion I applied for.
I just got hired at Anthropic as an ML engineer.
I got promoted from Senior to Staff Engineer.
I won Best Paper at CVPR this year.
After 10 years at IBM, I'm leaving to take care of my aging father.
I quit my tech job to open a bakery.
My startup failed. We're shutting down after 3 years.
We raised $15M Series B from Benchmark.
My co-founder and I are splitting up because we disagree on the direction.
Our app crossed 1 million monthly active users.
I pushed a bug to production that took down the service for 2 hours.
I was out sick with the flu for a week and missed a deadline.
My wife and I are having a baby in October.
I'm moving to Tokyo for a 2-year assignment.
I finished my first marathon in 4:32.
I just published my first novel on Amazon KDP.
I dropped out of my PhD after 3 years.
I finally finished a side project I'd been putting off for 8 months.
I forgot to reply to an important client email for 3 weeks.
en_to_linkedin_hard: My asshole landlord just raised my rent 30% with no warning. I have 60 days to figure out what to do.
I finished a big project at work, had a fight with my brother, went to the dentist, and decided to start running again — all in one week.

After 4 years of working nights and weekends at my day job as an accountant, I finally finished building a spreadsheet automation tool that cuts monthly close from 14 days to 3 days. I've been using it internally for 6 months and it's saved my team 40 hours a week. I'm thinking about turning it into a SaaS product but I'm scared of leaving my steady job, dealing with taxes and incorporation, and I don't know if anyone would pay for it. My wife thinks I should do it. My father thinks I'm nuts.
linkedin_to_en: Thrilled beyond words to share that after 5 incredible years at Meta, I've decided to embark on a new chapter. I'm now officially exploring new opportunities and #OpenToWork. To my amazing manager, my incredible team, and the mentors who shaped me — this journey would not have been possible without you. The best is yet to come. #NewBeginnings #Grateful

After 8 amazing years at Netflix, today is my last day. I've decided to take a year off to travel, write, and reconnect with what matters most. Huge thanks to my incredible manager and the best team I've ever worked with. This isn't goodbye — it's see you later. #Grateful #NewJourney

Life has a funny way of redirecting you when you need it most. After 4 years at my previous company, I'm taking this moment to reflect, recalibrate, and reconnect with what truly lights me up. The best is yet to come. #NewChapter #Grateful

I'm humbled and honored to share that I've been promoted to Director of Product at Stripe! When I started here 6 years ago as a PM, I never imagined this day would come. Huge thanks to my incredible team who make every hard problem feel solvable. This is just the beginning. Dream big. Work hard. Onwards. #Promoted #Leadership #Grateful

I can't believe I'm saying this, but I've been named to the Forbes 30 Under 30 list this year. This honor belongs to every mentor, every teammate, and every person who ever told me 'no' and made me prove them wrong. Stay hungry. Stay humble. The real work starts now. #Forbes30Under30 #Grateful #Leadership

Beyond thrilled to share that we just closed our $22M Series B led by Andreessen Horowitz, with participation from Sequoia and every angel who believed in us from day 1. Building a company is the hardest and most rewarding thing I've ever done. The journey is just beginning. #Funding #Startups #GrowthMindset

Today I want to share something difficult. After 18 months of pouring our hearts into this, we've made the painful decision to wind down Acme AI. Here's what this journey taught me: 1. Every no is a redirection. 2. Your team is your real asset. 3. Failure is just feedback. To everyone who believed in us — thank you. Onwards and upwards. #Founder #Lessons #GrowthMindset

Big news! I'm stepping out of my VP role to lead our new Individual Contributor track as Principal Engineer. After much reflection, I realized what truly drives me is building — not just managing. Life is too short to optimize for titles. I'm grateful to leadership for supporting this move and excited to get back to my roots. #Engineering #Growth #Leadership

Beyond excited to share that I'm joining OpenAI as Head of Developer Relations starting next month! After 6 years at Google Cloud, I felt a calling to work on the most transformative technology of our lifetime. See you all in SF. #NewRole #AI #OpenAI

Your comfort zone will kill your dreams faster than any failure ever could. Every giant started as a beginner. Every expert was once an amateur. Every winner was once a quitter who refused to quit. What separates the 1% from the 99%? Relentless belief. Show up. Do the work. Repeat. #Mindset #Hustle #Success

I had the most profound conversation with my Uber driver this morning. He told me he's been driving for 12 years to put his 3 kids through college. Two are now doctors, one is a lawyer. In a world obsessed with shortcuts, he reminded me that quiet consistency beats loud ambition every time. Sometimes the greatest leadership lessons come from where you least expect them. #Humility #Leadership #Gratitude

Here are 10 things I wish I had known at 22: 1. Your network is your net worth. 2. Save before you spend. 3. Invest in yourself first. 4. Read every day. 5. Say yes to hard things. 6. Your mindset is your superpower. 7. Rejection is redirection. 8. Health is wealth. 9. Build in public. 10. Never stop learning. Which one resonates most with you? #Growth #CareerAdvice

Unpopular opinion: 'Hustle culture' is dead, and that's a good thing. The new winners aren't grinding 80 hours a week. They're working smart, protecting their energy, and compounding small wins over years. Productivity without sustainability is just slow self-destruction. #WorkLifeBalance #Leadership #Productivity

I've been thinking a lot about AI lately. Here's the uncomfortable truth most leaders are afraid to say: if you're not using AI every day in 2026, your career has a ticking clock. The gap between AI-native operators and everyone else is widening by the week. I'm not saying this to scare you. I'm saying this because I care. #AI #FutureOfWork

Just got back from 2 weeks in Bali and I have one question for you: when was the last time you truly disconnected? Not 'checking email once a day' disconnected. Fully. Off. I came back with more clarity than I've had in 2 years. Rest isn't laziness. Rest is strategy. #Wellbeing #Leadership #Mindset

I just finished 'Atomic Habits' by James Clear and I can confidently say it's one of the 5 books that has fundamentally changed how I think about work, leadership, and life. My top 3 takeaways: 1. You don't rise to your goals — you fall to your systems. 2. 1% better every day compounds. 3. Identity > outcomes. Drop your favorite read in the comments. #Books #Leadership #Growth

Reminder: the best opportunities of your career will come from people you haven't met yet. This week I had 3 meaningful conversations that could shape the next chapter of my work — every one of them started with someone being brave enough to send a cold message. Your DMs are open, right? Mine are. #Networking #CareerAdvice

Spent the last 3 days at Web Summit in Lisbon and I'm genuinely inspired. Quick recap: 70,000 attendees, 30+ keynotes watched, 100+ new connections, 1 unforgettable beach dinner with a founder I've been following for years. Huge thanks to the Web Summit team for putting together an unforgettable event. Who else was there? #WebSummit #Networking #Tech

This morning my 4-year-old daughter asked me why I was working on my laptop during breakfast. I didn't have a good answer. So I closed it. In that moment she taught me more about presence, priorities, and leadership than any MBA course ever could. Sometimes the greatest wisdom comes in the smallest voice. Put the laptop down. #Parenting #Leadership #Presence

For the first time in my career, I'm going to be honest about something I've been hiding. Last year I experienced severe burnout. I couldn't get out of bed for weeks. I was embarrassed, ashamed, and scared people would see me as weak. I'm sharing this now because if even one person reading this is struggling in silence — you are not alone. Your productivity does not equal your worth. Reach out. Talk to someone. The strongest thing you can do is ask for help. #MentalHealth #Leadership #Vulnerability
linkedin_to_en_hard: Today I cried in an airport terminal. A woman in a pink sweater asked if I was okay. I told her my wife had just left me, my company had missed its quarterly targets by 34%, and our lead investor pulled out of the next round. She didn't say anything. She just sat next to me for 45 minutes. Didn't know my name. Didn't know my title. Didn't care about the $450M we had raised. She just sat there. When I finally stood up to board my flight to Davos, she handed me a tissue and said, 'Everyone needs someone.' That moment taught me more about leadership than 20 years of Harvard executive education. We don't need more MBAs. We need more humans. #Leadership #Vulnerability #HumanConnection

Yesterday I fired my highest-performing sales rep. She had crushed her quota 4 quarters in a row. She was on track to be our top earner. But she treated our junior staff like garbage. She rolled her eyes in meetings. She refused to mentor anyone under her. She was brilliant. She was also toxic. I walked into her office at 4:15pm on a Friday and I let her go. Was it hard? Yes. Was it expensive? Absolutely. Will we miss her numbers? 100%. But culture isn't a poster on a wall. Culture is what you do when it costs you. #Leadership #CultureMatters #HardDecisions

I was running late for a VC meeting in Palo Alto. I jumped into an Uber, pulled out my laptop, and started rehearsing my pitch. My driver — let's call him Marcus — watched me in the mirror. About 5 minutes in, he said, 'You know what makes a great company?' I looked up, annoyed. I was trying to finalize my 3-year forecasts. 'People,' he said. 'Just people.' I didn't think much of it at the time. But I landed the $4.5M round that afternoon. I've thought about Marcus every single day since. Sometimes the wisdom you need isn't in the boardroom. It's in the driver's seat. If you're reading this, Marcus — thank you. #StartupLife #Wisdom #Humility

Last month I turned down a $1.8M job offer from a company whose name you would absolutely recognize. Five minutes in the CEO's office. Corner view. Compensation that would have set my family up for a decade. And I said no. Why? Because the offer came with a phrase I couldn't shake: 'We need you to be our fixer.' I didn't spend 18 years building my career to be anyone's fixer. I spent it to be a builder. So I said no. And I woke up the next morning lighter than I had felt in years. Money isn't the prize. Alignment is. #Leadership #Alignment #CareerChoices

My dog Biscuit died on Tuesday. He was 14. A rescue pittie with one missing ear and the softest heart I have ever known. As I sat with him in the vet's office, something hit me that I have been wrestling with ever since. Biscuit never missed a deadline. He never worried about his runway. He never agonized over quarterly reports. He just showed up, every single day, and loved the people in front of him. How much of our 80-hour workweeks would Biscuit be proud of? I don't have the answer. But I'm asking the question. Rest in peace, buddy. You were a better leader than most CEOs I know. #DogDad #Leadership #WhatMatters

From 0 to $4.2M ARR in 11 months. No VC money. No growth hacks. No paid ads. Just 3 things: 1. We obsessed over the customer. Every. Single. Conversation. 2. We said no to 87% of the deals we could have closed. 3. We shipped every Friday, even when it was bad. People keep asking me how we did it. The truth is: we didn't optimize for growth. We optimized for truth. And growth was the byproduct. If you're building something right now and it feels slow — you're probably closer than you think. #Bootstrapped #SaaS #Founder

Today was the hardest day of my career. I had to let go of 42 people I respect, admire, and consider family. I will carry their names with me. I will remember every face in that Zoom call. If you are one of the 42 — my heart is with you. We will do everything in our power to help you land somewhere worthy of your talent. If you are hiring right now and you're reading this: DM me. I will personally vouch for every single one. To my remaining team — we are going to build something worthy of what we just lost. To everyone outside our company — please hire from this group. They are exceptional. #Layoffs #Leadership

During my MBA at Wharton, a professor once asked our class a single question: 'Who here wants to build a billion-dollar company?' Every hand shot up. Then he said: 'Who here wants to serve ten thousand people?' Three hands went up. I still think about that moment 14 years later. I run a company that serves over 200,000 customers now. Our revenue is meaningful. Our team is incredible. But I never forget: we were never here for the number. We were here for the people. The number is the receipt. The mission is the transaction. #Leadership #Mission #Purpose
Your 'quiet quitting' is not a trend. It is an early signal that your leadership has failed to earn followership. Stop blaming Gen Z. Start examining your mirror. #Leadership #Management

Last week I hired a 19-year-old with no degree, no internships, and no LinkedIn connections to run marketing for my portfolio company. Everyone on my team thought I was insane. Her pitch deck had typos. Her code samples were average. Her Figma files were a mess. But when I asked her one question — 'Why should I hire you?' — she looked me dead in the eye and said: 'Because I will care more than anyone you've ever met.' That sentence was worth more than any credential in the stack. Three months in, she has outperformed everyone. Credentials are rented. Character is owned. #Hiring #Talent #Leadership
